name: Acoustic Guitar
subtitle: Steel-string model
description: The acoustic guitar is a versatile and popular string instrument known for its rich warm tone. Ideal for various genres, from folk to rock, this steel-string model offers excellent projection and a comfortable playability for both beginners and professionals.

name: Electric Piano
subtitle: 88-key keyboard
description: A full-weighted 88-key electric piano delivering authentic acoustic piano feel with modern digital versatility. Features multiple voices, recording capabilities, and connectivity options for both studio and live performance.

name: Violin
subtitle: Classical string instrument
description: A handcrafted classical violin built from tonewoods including spruce top and maple back. Produces the warm, expressive tone characteristic of fine orchestral instruments. Suitable for classical, folk, and contemporary music.

name: Drum Kit
subtitle: Full drum set
description: A complete 5-piece acoustic drum kit with bass drum, snare, three toms, and hi-hat. Constructed with birch shells for a bright, cutting sound. Includes all hardware and a matching snare drum.

name: Saxophone
subtitle: Brass wind instrument
description: An alto saxophone crafted from high-quality brass with a gold lacquer finish. Renowned for its expressive, vocal-like tone, the saxophone is central to jazz, blues, and classical music. Includes a quality mouthpiece and carrying case.

name: Trumpet
subtitle: Brass wind instrument
description: A professional Bb trumpet with a yellow brass bell and silver-plated finish. Delivers a bright, powerful tone ideal for jazz, orchestral, and solo performance. Monel valves ensure smooth, responsive action.

name: Classical Guitar
subtitle: Nylon-string model
description: A traditional Spanish classical guitar with a cedar top and rosewood back and sides. The nylon strings produce a warm, mellow tone perfect for classical, flamenco, and fingerstyle playing. Hand-built with exceptional craftsmanship.

name: Electric Guitar
subtitle: Solid body electric
description: A versatile solid-body electric guitar featuring humbucker and single-coil pickups for a wide range of tones. From clean jazz to heavy distortion, this instrument handles any style with ease. Includes a comfortable contoured body.

name: Cello
subtitle: Orchestral string instrument
description: A full-size cello with a spruce top and maple back, delivering deep, resonant tones. The cello's rich, human-like voice makes it central to chamber music and orchestral performance. Includes a quality bow and padded case.

name: Flute
subtitle: Silver concert flute
description: A professional concert flute crafted from silver-plated brass. Features closed-hole keys and an offset G key for comfortable playability. Produces a clear, bright tone across three octaves, ideal for orchestral and solo performance.

name: Djembe
subtitle: West African hand drum
description: A traditional West African djembe carved from a single piece of hardwood with a goat skin head. Capable of producing a wide range of tones from deep bass to sharp slap. Central to West African music and increasingly popular worldwide.

name: Upright Bass
subtitle: Double bass
description: A full-size double bass providing deep, foundational tones for orchestra and jazz ensembles. Built with a spruce top and maple back, it offers both arco and pizzicato versatility. The cornerstone of any serious string section.

name: Oboe
subtitle: Double-reed woodwind
description: A professional orchestral oboe made from grenadilla wood. Its distinctive nasal, penetrating tone gives the oboe a unique voice in the orchestra. The instrument traditionally provides the tuning A for the entire ensemble.

name: Marimba
subtitle: Keyboard percussion
description: A 4.3-octave concert marimba with rosewood bars and aluminium resonators. The marimba produces warm, mellow tones across a wide range. A cornerstone of percussion ensemble and solo repertoire worldwide.

name: Banjo
subtitle: 5-string bluegrass banjo
description: A 5-string resonator banjo with a maple neck and a 11-inch head. Its bright, cutting tone is synonymous with American bluegrass and folk music. Features a planetary tuner set for precise and stable tuning.

name: Synthesizer
subtitle: Analog synthesizer
description: A 37-key analog synthesizer with a paraphonic architecture and classic filter design. Capable of producing rich pads, sharp leads, and deep bass sounds. Features MIDI and USB connectivity for modern studio integration.

name: Harp
subtitle: Concert pedal harp
description: A 47-string concert pedal harp with a spruce soundboard and a rich maple pillar. The seven pedals allow full chromatic playing across six and a half octaves. The harp's ethereal tone has made it an orchestral and solo favourite for centuries.

name: Mandolin
subtitle: Bluegrass mandolin
description: An F-style mandolin with a solid spruce top and maple back and sides. The bright, punchy tone is a hallmark of bluegrass and American folk music. Eight strings in four pairs provide a full, resonant sound.

name: Trombone
subtitle: Tenor trombone
description: A professional tenor trombone with a yellow brass bell and a lightweight nickel silver slide. Delivers a full, rich tone across a wide dynamic range. Equally at home in orchestral, jazz big band, and solo settings.

name: Tabla
subtitle: Indian hand drums
description: A pair of traditional Indian hand drums consisting of the dayan (treble drum) and bayan (bass drum). The tabla produces a wide tonal palette through intricate hand techniques. Central to Hindustani classical music and Indian popular music.

name: Sitar
subtitle: North Indian lute
description: A traditional North Indian sitar with 18-20 strings, sympathetic strings providing its distinctive resonant drone. Carved from teak and tun wood, the sitar is the dominant instrument in Hindustani classical music. Complex and beautiful.

name: Bass Guitar
subtitle: 4-string electric bass
description: A 4-string electric bass guitar with an alder body and a maple neck. Produces the deep, driving low-end that underpins every band. Split-coil and single-coil pickups offer a wide range of tones from punchy to smooth.

name: Accordion
subtitle: Piano accordion
description: A 120-bass piano accordion with 41 treble keys and a rich, full sound. Essential in French musette, tango, zydeco, and folk traditions worldwide. The bellows-driven reed mechanism produces a characteristic, expressive sound.

name: Xylophone
subtitle: Orchestral mallet instrument
description: A 3.5-octave orchestral xylophone with rosewood bars and a bright, dry tone. The xylophone provides rhythmic and melodic punctuation in orchestral and band settings. A staple of percussion education and professional performance.

name: Balalaika
subtitle: Russian triangular lute
description: A prima balalaika with a triangular spruce body and three strings. The national instrument of Russia, producing a distinctive bright, twangy tone. Used in Russian folk ensembles and increasingly in contemporary music.

name: Cajon
subtitle: Percussion box drum
description: A solid birch cajon with a snare mechanism producing a full range of percussive tones. Originating in Peru and refined in Spain, the cajon has become the standard percussion instrument in flamenco and acoustic band settings.

name: Harmonica
subtitle: 10-hole blues harp
description: A 10-hole diatonic harmonica in the key of C with stainless steel cover plates and a brass reed plate. The harmonica is essential to blues, folk, and country music. Portable and expressive, it rewards both beginners and masters.

name: Dulcimer
subtitle: Appalachian mountain dulcimer
description: An Appalachian mountain dulcimer with a walnut body and a spruce soundboard. This fretted lap instrument has a gentle, drone-based sound tied to American folk traditions. Easy to learn and deeply expressive in the right hands.

name: Bongo Drums
subtitle: Afro-Cuban hand drums
description: A pair of traditional Afro-Cuban bongo drums featuring rawhide heads and hardwood shells. Played between the knees, bongos provide the rhythmic foundation of salsa, son, and Latin jazz. Expressive and immediate in feel.

name: Bassoon
subtitle: Double-reed orchestral woodwind
description: A professional orchestral bassoon crafted from maple with a curved bocal. The bassoon covers the lowest range in the woodwind family and produces a characterful, rich tone with remarkable agility across its four-octave range.

name: Steel Guitar
subtitle: Pedal steel guitar
description: A 10-string single-neck pedal steel guitar with three pedals and four knee levers. The sliding steel bar and pedal/lever system allow smooth pitch changes and a uniquely expressive, singing tone central to country music.

name: Kora
subtitle: West African harp-lute
description: A 21-string kora built from a large calabash resonator covered in cowhide. The kora's shimmering, harp-like tone is the defining sound of West African griot music. Played with thumbs and index fingers in a complex, interlocking style.

name: Piccolo
subtitle: Half-size concert flute
description: A silver-plated piccolo, the smallest and highest-pitched member of the flute family. Produces a piercing, brilliant tone that cuts through the full orchestra or band. Demanding technically but electrifying in its upper register.

name: Vibraphone
subtitle: Motor-driven mallet instrument
description: A 3-octave vibraphone with aluminium bars and motor-driven rotating discs that create a characteristic vibrato effect. The vibraphone is essential to jazz harmony and modern percussion ensemble writing. Sustain is controlled via a damper pedal.

name: Chapman Stick
subtitle: Two-handed tapping instrument
description: A 12-string Chapman Stick played entirely by tapping both hands on the fretboard simultaneously. Bass and melody lines can be played independently, making it a one-person rhythm section. A unique and expressive electric instrument.

name: Tambourine
subtitle: Frame drum with jingles
description: A traditional tambourine with a wooden frame, a synthetic head, and brass jingles. Produces a lively, bright sound when struck or shaken. Used in orchestral, pop, folk, and sacred music across many world traditions.

name: Theremin
subtitle: Electronic proximity instrument
description: The theremin is the only instrument played without physical contact. Two antennas sense the position of the player's hands, controlling pitch and volume. Its eerie, singing tone made it the first electronic instrument and a sci-fi soundtrack staple.

name: Oud
subtitle: Middle Eastern lute
description: A fretless 11-string oud with a pear-shaped spruce body. The ancestor of the European lute, the oud is the central instrument of Arabic, Turkish, and Persian classical music. Its deep, warm tone and expressive microtonal range are unmatched.

name: Steel Drum
subtitle: Caribbean pan drum
description: A soprano steelpan crafted from a repurposed oil drum. The national instrument of Trinidad and Tobago, the steelpan covers nearly three octaves with a bright, singing tone. Central to Caribbean carnival music and increasingly prominent in contemporary settings.

name: Bouzouki
subtitle: Greek long-neck lute
description: A trichordo bouzouki with three pairs of strings and a long, fretted neck. The defining instrument of Greek rebetiko music, producing a bright, metallic tone with a distinctive buzzy quality. Also widely used in Irish traditional music.

name: Guiro
subtitle: Latin American scraper
description: A traditional guiro carved from a dried gourd with parallel notches scraped with a stick. Produces a distinctive rasping, rhythmic sound essential to salsa, merengue, and cumbia. Simple to play yet vital to the rhythmic texture of Latin music.

name: Erhu
subtitle: Chinese two-string fiddle
description: A traditional Chinese erhu with a hexagonal resonator body and two silk strings played with a horsehair bow. The erhu's expressive, plaintive voice is central to Chinese classical, folk, and operatic music. Capable of remarkable emotional depth.

name: Lute
subtitle: Renaissance plucked string
description: A 7-course Renaissance lute with a spruce soundboard and a distinctive vaulted back of alternating maple and rosewood ribs. The lute was the dominant instrument of the Renaissance and produces a subtle, intimate tone perfect for solo repertoire.

name: Congas
subtitle: Afro-Cuban tall drums
description: A set of three congas — quinto, conga, and tumba — with rawhide heads and hardwood stave shells. Essential to Afro-Cuban music, salsa, and Latin jazz, congas are played with the hands and fingers to produce a wide range of tones and textures.

name: Drum Machine
subtitle: Electronic beat sequencer
description: A modern drum machine with 64 pads, a built-in step sequencer, and hundreds of sampled and synthesized drum sounds. Foundational to electronic music, hip-hop, and pop production. Features MIDI, USB, and CV/Gate connectivity.
